frame_analyses.description: Red foam organ with three appendages visible on green drape. No instruments yet visible. Task setup stage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS endoloop trainer setup with three distinct appendages

frame_analyses.description: Gloved hand introduces pre-tied endoloop applicator from outside trainer. Loop applicator held in right hand with blue handle visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper introduction of loop device. Applicator orientation appears correct for target approach.

frame_analyses.description: Endoloop applicator positioned horizontally above the foam organ. Device held steady for navigation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control and positioning. Loop ready to be advanced toward appendage.

frame_analyses.description: Endoloop applicator advanced to center appendage. Loop visible extending from applicator tip. Attempting to position loop around middle appendage base.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate target selection (middle appendage). Loop deployment initiated.

frame_analyses.description: Loop actively positioned and opened around the target appendage. Applicator held steady while loop encircles appendage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth loop deployment. Good positioning relative to appendage base mark.

frame_analyses.description: Jumbo clip instrument introduced. Grasper visible in frame holding or stabilizing appendage while pusher clip handles loop advancement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Introduction of cinching device. Proper two-instrument coordination for knot tightening.

frame_analyses.description: Jumbo clip/pusher actively advancing loop down the appendage. Loop cinching in progress. Clear compression of appendage tissue visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Active loop advancement and cinching. Appropriate tension being applied.

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully cinched and secured at appendage base. Knot position appears to align with marked location. Loop tightly seated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop appears well-positioned at the target mark. Cinching complete.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic scissors introduced. Positioned to cut the tail of the loop material. Scissors properly oriented.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors inserted for tail cutting phase. Proper instrument selection and orientation.

frame_analyses.description: Tail of loop material cut with scissors. Clean cut visible. Loop remains secured at appendage base during cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tail cut performed cleanly. Loop integrity maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view shows completed endoloop on appendage. Loop securely cinched at marked location. All instruments removed from field. Task appears complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final laparoscopic confirmation view. Loop position and security visible. Appendage integrity maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Extended laparoscopic view of ligated appendage. Multiple appendages visible. Loop-secured appendage shows complete cinching with no slippage. Final state documented.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion confirmed. Loop secure, appendage functional integrity preserved (no transection).

penalties.description: Loop knot position appears approximately 2mm proximal to the marked line on the appendage base. Visible compression point is slightly higher than the target mark.

loop_deployment.notes: Target appendage correctly selected and looped.

knot_security.notes: Loop firmly seated and does not appear to slip. Cinching complete and stable.

pusher_break.notes: Pusher break occurred at appropriate location outside trainer.

tail_cut.notes: Clean tail cut with loop remaining secure.

appendage_integrity.notes: No visible tearing or destructive compression. Appendage remains intact.

confidence_rationale: Completion time and task phases clearly visible throughout video. Loop deployment, cinching, and cutting all performed successfully with good instrument control. Minor uncertainty regarding exact mm deviation at knot mark due to laparoscopic camera angle and lighting variation; estimated conservatively at 2mm. All critical task components completed successfully.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation from target mark—laparoscopic perspective and lighting make precise measurement difficult; estimate based on visual relationship to marked location
technique_summary: Competent intermediate performance. Trainee successfully deployed loop, positioned it on the correct appendage, cinched it at approximately the marked location, broke the pusher appropriately, and cut the tail cleanly. Completion time of 58 seconds is slightly above the expert benchmark of 53 seconds but well within intermediate range. No tissue damage or loop slippage observed. Minor positional deviation from exact mark.
strengths: Clean, secure knot cinching without slippage
Clean tail cut while maintaining loop security
No appendage transection or tissue damage
improvement_suggestions: Reduce loop positioning time to achieve expert-level speed (target <53 seconds)
Refine mark alignment—ensure knot positioned precisely at the provided mark, not proximal to it
Practice smoother transitions between phases to eliminate minor time inefficiencies
